summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Gogs 0.14.3 in Development mode exposes detailed stack traces, internal paths, and runtime information through error responses. Sending malformed multipart POST requests to /user/sign_up and /user/login triggers panics that return HTTP 500 responses containing full stack traces with internal module paths.
expected_security_impact: Error responses should expose stack traces, internal paths, and runtime details that facilitate attacker reconnaissance. Malformed multipart requests should trigger panics that disclose Go runtime internals and application module paths.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the gogs/gogs:latest image, started Gogs 0.14.3 in Development mode, and sent malformed multipart POST requests to /user/sign_up and /user/login. Both returned HTTP 500 with full panic stack traces including: 'runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference', runtime/panic.go:262, runtime/signal_unix.go:925, github.com/go-macaron/binding paths, and gopkg.in/macaron.v1 internal paths.
verification_method: The exploit sends malformed multipart requests to trigger panic responses and checks for disclosure patterns in the response body: goroutine stack traces, Go runtime paths (runtime/), internal module paths (gogs.io/gogs/internal, /app/gogs/), panic messages. The verify.sh script checks for a proof file that records all disclosed information.
evidence_produced_by_run: POST /user/sign_up (malformed multipart) returned HTTP 500 with full stack trace
POST /user/login (invalid multipart) returned HTTP 500 with full stack trace
Panic message: 'runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference'
Gogs running in Development mode (non-production) which enables error disclosure

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
